reasoning: Ukraine is currently under martial law, which prohibits holding an election and any formal announcement of one. Under Ukrainian law and the constitution, elections can only be officially announced after martial law is lifted. On April 16, 2025, the Ukrainian parliament extended martial law for an additional 90 days, keeping it in force until August 6, 2025. This means that martial law will remain in effect throughout the rest of the timeline specified in the resolution criteria (before July 1, 2025). Furthermore, while there have been peace talks between Russia and Ukraine in mid-May 2025, there is no finalized ceasefire agreement, only "technical negotiations" following limited outcomes, meaning an end to the war before July 1 is practically impossible. President Zelenskyy has explicitly stated that elections will only be announced *when* martial law is lifted. Additionally, the President's office explicitly denied rumors about preparing for elections on April 2, 2025. Therefore, a formal announcement of a specific date for a presidential election before July 1, 2025 by the Ukrainian government is extremely unlikely. The probability is near the absolute floor.
